goal: Phase 4: per-screen CSS themes scoped to each reading-mode page. Five new files — css/daily.css, css/question.css, css/reading.css, css/full.css, css/profile-theme.css — each scoped to its body[data-page] attribute, referencing --mt-* tokens only, loading after css/phase-3-redesign.css to win the cascade.
done: Created five page-scoped CSS files following the same protocol per file: (a) grep the relevant JS render function(s) to enumerate the actually-injected class names; (b) write the file with sectioned rules targeting only confirmed classes; (c) link the file as the LAST css link on each HTML page that hosts that data-page value; (d) cp canonical → www/ and cap sync ios. All rules use the --mt-* token namespace (zero hardcoded hex). Files: css/daily.css (174L, 7 sections: page bg, panel surfaces, card-art frame, selection-board states, meta badges + swatch, typography, retention+phase panels) → daily.html, th/daily.html. css/question.css (4 sections: page bg, topic chip-cards, spread selector pills, question-draw selection-board states) → question.html, th/question.html, question-draw.html, th/question-draw.html. css/reading.css (9 sections: page bg, panel surfaces, reading intro, spread layout COLOR-ONLY for celtic-cross compat, question takeaway, energy balance, meta+typography, retention+phase, action row + footer pills) → reading.html, th/reading.html. css/full.css (6 sections, STRICT color/typography only — never touch layout/position/z-index per spec, since the celtic-cross selection + arrange UI has complex positioning in styles.css) → full.html, th/full.html. css/profile-theme.css (7 sections: page bg, panel surfaces, reading intro, streak progress bar (--mt-streak-bar gradient headline), deck inventory hint, history items, empty state) → profile.html, th/profile.html.
affects: daily.html + th/daily.html — daily-mode result page now uses --mt-* pastel surface treatment for .panel, .card-art, meta-badges, typography, retention CTAs
question.html + th/question.html — topic chip-cards and Quick/Story spread selector pills re-skinned with --mt-* tokens

question-draw.html + th/question-draw.html — first time these pages receive the Phase 3 CSS chain (theme-tokens, phase-2-header, phase-2-1-logo, phase-3-redesign); previously they linked only styles.css. Phase 3.2 .card-slot fallback gradient now actually applies on the question-draw selection board (it was dead code on this page before today). Plus the new question.css selection-board states.

reading.html + th/reading.html — result page (used by all three modes via renderDaily/renderQuestion/renderFull) gets full surface re-skin including question-takeaway panel, energy balance, spread cards, retention + phase panels, footer nav pills

full.html + th/full.html — celtic-cross 10-card selection + arrange UI gets color/shadow treatment for full-draw-board slots (is-picked-confirm gold glow, is-awaiting-card rose glow), full-celtic-preview slot states (is-filled / is-latest / is-next), and full-arrange-item drag states — strictly color-only to preserve the existing positioning

profile.html + th/profile.html — profile page gets streak-progress-bar with --mt-streak-bar gold-rose gradient (the headline visual), panel surfaces, history items with hover lift, empty state treatment
notes: PHASE 3 GAP FIXED: question-draw.html and th/question-draw.html were never included in the original Phase 3 12-page link list (the user's Phase 3 spec listed index/daily/question/reading/full/profile but omitted question-draw). They were loading only styles.css. The body[data-page='question-draw'] .card-slot rule from phase-3-redesign.css §2 has been dead code since Phase 3 shipped on 2026-05-28. Backfilled the full Phase 3 chain (theme-tokens, phase-2-header, phase-2-1-logo, phase-3-redesign) to both question-draw pages this commit. STRICT FULL.CSS DISCIPLINE: per spec, full.css uses only color, background, border-color (not border shorthand), box-shadow, text-color properties; no width / height / padding / margin / display / grid-* / flex-* / position / z-index / transform / border-width / font-size / line-height changes anywhere in that file. Reason: full.html's celtic-cross selection + arrange UI has interactive layout logic in css/styles.css that we should not destabilize. No JS files touched in Phase 4 (CSS + HTML link insertion only); node --check not run. Visual QA against meowtarot.com required after push.
verified: grep '<link.*\.css' confirms the new theme is the LAST css link on every targeted page: daily.css on daily/th-daily, question.css on question/th-question/question-draw/th-question-draw, reading.css on reading/th-reading, full.css on full/th-full, profile-theme.css on profile/th-profile
md5 canonical == www == ios for all 5 new css files plus all 12 modified HTML files
cap sync ios completed once per file (5 runs, each 0.10-0.14s, no plugin errors)

all class selectors targeted are confirmed present in js/main.js or js/reading.js or js/profile.js or js/achievements-panel.js or js/deck-inventory.js grep output, with the file each class came from documented in the CSS file header
every rule references --mt-* tokens; grep '#[0-9a-fA-F]' on the 5 new css files returns zero hex literal matches
filed: Deck-reward popup theme — .mt-dr-overlay from js/deck-reward.js is a global modal that can fire on any page (streak milestone unlock). Not scoped to data-page='profile'. Belongs in a separate phase that themes global overlays.
